plan: Trim SPY to 20% to meet concentration limits and reduce overall risk.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure.
Increase QQQI to 25% and QQQ to 20% for stable broad market and income exposure.
Buy XLV at 10% to diversify into a defensive sector.
Maintain a significant cash buffer by reducing overall invested capital.
This plan prioritizes capital preservation and reduces overall portfolio volatility.
conservative.read: This conservative approach targets a modest ~1.5%/month return, focusing on capital preservation. Expect lower volatility and smaller drawdowns, but also limited upside potential. Risk is significantly reduced by cutting leverage and diversifying into more stable assets.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits and reduce gross leverage.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to reduce leveraged ETF exposure.
Increase QQQI to 22% to maintain a good income component.
Buy RTX at 8% and AMAT at 8% to add high-quality long setups.
This plan balances growth with risk management, aiming for consistent returns.
A moderate cash buffer will be maintained for flexibility.
balanced.read: This balanced approach aims for ~2.5%/month growth, blending broad market exposure with quality tech. Risk is managed by reducing leverage and diversifying, but some market volatility should still be expected. Drawdowns are expected to be moderate.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits and reduce gross leverage.
Increase SOXL to 10% to lean into leveraged semiconductor exposure.
Increase CRWD to 20% to press a winning AI/Software position.
Buy RTX at 10%, AMAT at 10%, and NET at 5% to add high-scoring setups.
This plan uses leverage and concentration to maximize growth potential.
A smaller cash buffer is maintained, and strict stops are critical.
aggressive.read: This aggressive growth approach targets ~4%/month by leveraging high-conviction setups and increasing exposure to leveraged ETFs. Be aware that this strategy carries significantly higher risk, including potential for larger and faster drawdowns, and daily decay/rebalancing effects on leveraged ETFs. Strict stop discipline is paramount to manage risk.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits and reduce gross leverage.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure.
Increase QQQI to 35% to maximize income-generating exposure.
Trim QQQ to 10% and CRWD to 5% to reduce growth and high-beta exposure.
This plan prioritizes steady monthly distributions and low drawdown.
A cash buffer will be maintained for stability and opportunistic entries.
income.read: This income-focused approach targets ~1%/month through reliable distributions from income ETFs. While prioritizing low drawdown, NAV can still fall in market selloffs, and distributions are variable. This is not a savings account, but aims for cash-like income with reduced market volatility.
